Meta.title: Constitutional Map AI – Global Semantic Atlas of Constitutional Law
Meta.description: Compare constitutional texts from 190+ countries on an interactive 3D semantic map. Explore rights, institutions, and legal language across the world's constitutions.
Meta.ogImageAlt: Constitutional Map AI – 3D semantic visualization of global constitutional law

Chrome.subtitle: A global semantic map of constitutional law
Chrome.primerP1: Each point in the 3D view represents a **constitutional article** or other meaningful legal unit. Nearby points are not nearby because they come from the same country, but because the language of those passages is **semantically similar**. Use **country selection** to compare how different constitutions occupy the same semantic terrain.
Chrome.primerP2: The **map** and the **country list** are selection tools. They decide which constitutions are loaded into the scene. Selecting more countries does not change the geometry of the embedding itself; it changes which parts of that global semantic space you can inspect.
Chrome.primerP3: The **embedding** turns legal text into vectors, and the **clustering** step groups vectors that tend to discuss related constitutional themes. In **country mode**, color shows political origin. In **cluster mode**, color shows thematic neighborhood.
Chrome.primerP4: **Large, dense clouds** usually indicate recurring constitutional ideas such as rights, institutions, emergency powers, elections, or amendment rules. **Isolated points** often mark unusual provisions, rare wording, or country-specific constitutional design choices.
Chrome.primerP5: The platform offers two types of search: **keyword search** finds literal term occurrences, while **semantic search** retrieves conceptually nearby passages even without matching terms. Search results highlight regions of the semantic space in the 3D canvas, linking what you read to where it sits.
Chrome.primerP6: In the country statistics, **Coverage** measures how much of the global cluster landscape a constitution reaches. **Entropy** measures how evenly its segments are distributed across that landscape: high entropy suggests a broader semantic spread, while low entropy suggests concentration in fewer themes.
Chrome.primerP7: In the article detail panel, **Global Cluster** is the identifier of the thematic group assigned to that segment in the worldwide clustering. When the value is **-1**, it means the segment was **left outside the defined thematic groupings** in that global step. **Probability** indicates how confidently the clustering model placed that segment in that group: higher values mean a cleaner fit, while lower values usually mark more ambiguous or boundary cases.
Chrome.dataNotice: Constitution texts in this application are sourced from the Constitute Project and used under the Creative Commons Attribution-Non Commercial 3.0 Unported License.
Chrome.citationText: Elkins, Zachary, Tom Ginsburg, James Melton. Constitute: The World’s Constitutions to Read, Search, and Compare.
Chrome.sourceCodeText: Source code is open — contributions welcome.
Chrome.agentGuideText: Instructions for browser-based AI agents are available at
Chrome.aiDisclaimer: Because this project used AI, Constitutional Map may contain segmentation, clustering, or content errors. If accuracy matters, consult official constitutional sources.
Chrome.supportText: This project runs on AI embeddings and cloud servers. Keeping the constitutional corpus current requires recurring pipeline runs and API costs. If you find it useful, consider sponsoring a month of infrastructure:

Atlas.countryLoadError: Country data could not be loaded for {country}.

WorldMap.title: Select constitutional systems on the map
WorldMap.body: Click any country with data to load its semantic points. Drag to pan and use the zoom controls for regional comparisons.
WorldMap.selectedCount: {count, plural, =0 {No countries selected} one {# country selected} other {# countries selected}}
WorldMap.selectedCountry: {country} was added to the comparison set.
WorldMap.unavailableCountry: {country} is unavailable in the current dataset.
WorldMap.hint: Hover a country to see its availability.

ControlPanel.title: Build comparison sets quickly
ControlPanel.body: Presets are additive. The country list can be filtered by name and re-ordered for broad or focused exploration.
ControlPanel.filterPlaceholder: Filter countries by name, code or region

Search.title: Search terms
Search.placeholder: Try freedom of speech, emergency powers, labor rights...
Search.restrictToSelectedAndHighlight: Restrict search to the {count, plural, one {# selected country} other {# selected countries}} and highlight the results in the 3D map
Search.restrictToSelectedDisabled: Select one or more countries to restrict the search.
Search.empty: Run a search to see ranked article matches.
Search.fullTextUnavailable: The full segment text is temporarily unavailable.
Search.advancedSearchDesc: Use AND, OR and quotes for exact phrases. Group terms with parentheses.

SemanticSearch.title: Search concepts or ideas
SemanticSearch.placeholder: Try judicial independence, decentralization, social rights...
SemanticSearch.languageHint: Queries may be written in any language, but English usually works best against this corpus.
SemanticSearch.exampleQuery: how judges of the highest court are selected by political authorities
SemanticSearch.restrictToSelectedAndHighlight: Restrict retrieval to the {count, plural, one {# selected country} other {# selected countries}} and highlight the results in the 3D map
SemanticSearch.restrictToSelectedDisabled: Select one or more countries to restrict retrieval.
SemanticSearch.empty: Run a semantic search to inspect semantically nearby constitutional articles.
SemanticSearch.fullTextUnavailable: The full segment text is temporarily unavailable.

Canvas.title: Navigate the semantic cluster field
Canvas.countryFocusPlaceholder: Type a code or country name
Canvas.segmentFocusPlaceholder: Type an article or segment name
Canvas.segmentFocusLoading: Loading segments for the selected country...
Canvas.segmentFocusEmpty: No loaded segments available for this country yet.
Canvas.empty: Select one or more countries to render their constitutional segments in 3D.
Canvas.hoverHint: Hover a point to inspect its article and text snippet.
Canvas.doubleClickHint: Double-click a point to focus the camera on its cluster location.
Canvas.detailEmpty: Click a point in the 3D view to inspect the full constitutional article here.

Stats.title: Semantic coverage by selected country
Stats.empty: Select countries on the map or from the control panel to inspect their metrics.

Detail.title: Selected article detail
Detail.empty: Click a point in the canvas or choose a search result to inspect the full constitutional text here.

Export.combinedNote: Semantic results first, then unique keyword results (duplicates removed).

Share.modalSubtitle: Add context so recipients understand what they are looking at.
Share.titlePlaceholder: e.g. Judicial independence clauses across Asia
Share.observationPlaceholder: Describe what you found and why it is interesting…
Share.successMessage: Your shareable link is ready. Anyone with it will see this exact view and your note.
Share.errorRateLimit: Too many links created. Please wait a while before trying again.
Share.errorGeneric: Something went wrong. Please try again.
Share.popupCountries: {count, plural, one {# country selected} other {# countries selected}}

WelcomeOnboarding.title: Start in three steps
WelcomeOnboarding.intro: Explore constitutional texts from more than 190 countries on a 3D semantic map.
WelcomeOnboarding.step1Body: Load one country, a geopolitical bloc, or all available countries into the atlas.
WelcomeOnboarding.step2Body: Use keyword search for exact terms and semantic search for concepts or ideas.
WelcomeOnboarding.step3Body: Nearby points indicate constitutional segments with similar meaning. Open article details from a point or a search result.
WelcomeOnboarding.secondaryShare: Share a research view by link with a comment
WelcomeOnboarding.reopenHint: You can reopen this guide anytime from the header.

BlogTutorial.metaDescription: Short tutorials for learning how to navigate the semantic map, compare countries, and validate findings in the full constitutional text.
BlogTutorial.title: Blog tutorial
BlogTutorial.intro: Short entries for learning the atlas workflow faster: how to select countries, compare distributions in the 3D canvas, and validate a finding in the full constitutional text.
